query: InterleaveThinker complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Rituraj on X: "🚨 Researchers just gave every image generator on the planet a capability it was never designed to have. Not a new model. A two-agent pipeline that wraps around any existing generator and teaches it to produce mixed text-and-image sequences — visual narratives, step-by-step https://t.co/uzrwXuNzGe" / X
results.content: It doesn't generate anything — it organizes. A Critic agent evaluates every image the generator produces against the Planner's instructions. Did the image actually show what was asked? Does it match the narrative context? Is it consistent with the previous images in the sequence? If not — the Critic rewrites the specific instruction and sends it back for regeneration. Generate. Evaluate. Correct. Regenerate. Until the sequence is coherent. Here's the number that defines the result. On interleaved generation benchmarks, InterleaveThinker matches Nano Banana Pro and GPT-5. The two most capable closed-source systems in the world for this task. Not close. Matches. Here's the part most people will miss. The pipeline made the base image generators better at single-image tasks too. On WISE and [...] + image), here's the final result (image). Text and images woven together. Each image informed by the context of what came before. Each text section grounded in the specific images around it. Every current image generator produces one image per prompt. That's it. The architecture was never built for sequences. You can chain prompts manually — but the generator has no memory of what it produced three steps ago and no ability to correct itself when something drifts. InterleaveThinker fixes this with two agents working together. A Planner agent breaks your high-level instruction into a structured sequence. It maps out what text goes where, what image goes where, and what each image needs to show relative to everything before it. It doesn't generate anything — it organizes. A Critic agent [...] 🚨 Researchers just gave every image generator on the planet a capability it was never designed to have. Not a new model. A two-agent pipeline that wraps around any existing generator and teaches it to produce mixed text-and-image sequences — visual narratives, step-by-step ction sets — without changing a single weight. It's called InterleaveThinker. And it solves the one thing every image generator fundamentally cannot do. Here's what interleaved generation actually means. You ask an AI to create a cooking tutorial. A truly useful output isn't just photos. It's: here's the ingredients (image), here's what your dough should look like after 5 minutes of kneading (image), make sure the texture looks like this before you proceed (text + image), here's the final result (image). Text and images

results.title: InterleaveThinker: Reinforcing Agentic Interleaved Generation
results.content:
## Instructions for reporting errors

We are continuing to improve HTML versions of papers, and your feedback helps enhance accessibility and mobile
support. To report errors in the HTML that will help us improve conversion and rendering, choose any of the
methods listed below:

Tip: You can select the relevant text first, to include it in your report.

Our team has already identified the following issues. We appreciate your time reviewing and reporting rendering errors we
may not have found yet. Your efforts will help us improve the HTML versions for all readers, because disability
should not be a barrier to accessing research. Thank you for your continued support in championing open access for
all. [...] Limitations. Although adaptable to any image generator, our framework’s capacity is constrained by the base model’s generative prior. Consequently, it cannot generate concepts that were not included in the base generator’s training corpus. We further show the bad case about this in Fig 8 in Appendix.

## References

## Appendix A System Prompt

## Appendix B Bad Cases

We show the bad cases of FLUX.2-klein in Fig 8. For concept that the frozen image generator does not know, our framework could not fix it and the model even occurs color shift, which will not happen in in-domain situation. [...] in early steps lead to severe step-wise error accumulation, eventually ruining the final output. DuoGen Shi et al. (2026) simulates UMM by jointly tuning a VLM and a video generator. Despite improved performance, it suffers from visual over-reliance and is incompatible with arbitrary image generators. InterleaveThinker overcomes these limitations by decoupling planning and generation, preventing myopic reactions to local visual feedback.

results.title: Tired of AI image generators dropping the ball on complex prompts ...
results.content:
Tired of AI image generators dropping the ball on complex prompts? 🎨✨  
  
Multimodal models often suffer from visual over-reliance and compounding errors—but a new breakthrough is changing the game: \\InterleaveThinker\\.  
  
It completely decouples planning from execution using a dedicated Planner → Generator → Critic loop. By blocking intermediate visual feedback, it prevents myopic drift and keeps the AI locked onto your full prompt. 🧠  
  
🔧 Powered by GRPO with a dual-reward RL strategy, this architecture boosts reasoning scores (WISE +55%, RISE +116%) and matches top proprietary models on frozen backbones like FLUX.2-klein.  
  
📚 Perfect for AI researchers, ML engineers, and developers pushing the limits of multimodal generation.

results.title: InterleaveThinker: Reinforcing Agentic Interleaved Generation
results.content:
John: So, to wrap up, InterleaveThinker introduces a practical and effective multi-agent framework to bring long-horizon, interleaved generation to existing models. By separating the task into a global Planner that avoids short-sightedness and an iterative Critic that corrects errors step-by-step, it provides a robust solution to a challenging problem in multimodal AI. The key takeaway is that specialized, collaborative agents can often outperform a single, monolithic model, especially for complex, sequential tasks.

John: Thanks for listening. If you have any further questions, ask our AI assistant or drop a comment. [...] John: Precisely. Think of generating a visual cooking recipe or a storybook page by page. The model needs to process the current state, which includes both the instruction and the last image, to create the next one. This paper aims to give that capability to any existing, fixed image generator.

John: The core problem InterleaveThinker addresses is twofold. First, as I mentioned, powerful image generators like FLUX or Midjourney are architecturally limited to single outputs. On the other hand, Unified Multimodal Models, or UMMs, which can handle interleaved formats, often suffer from what the authors call 'visual over-reliance' and 'step-wise error accumulation.'

Noah: Can you clarify what 'visual over-reliance' means in this context? [...] John: That's a great question about the trade-offs. A unified framework like UniGRPO aims for end-to-end optimization, which can theoretically lead to a more synergistic policy. However, it's a much more complex and monolithic system to train. InterleaveThinker's decoupled design is its strength. It's model-agnostic, allowing it to leverage the rapid progress in standalone image generators. The paper's ablation studies support this design choice; when they tried to merge the Planner and Critic into a single agent, performance dropped significantly, validating the need for specialized roles to prevent the system from getting confused by its own visual outputs.
